creator: dumbbell@FreeBSD.org
text:
(In reply to Jimmy Olgeni from comment #3)
> The WX default is on the way with 17.4, let's start with that one - the Java
> dependency might be overkill for binary packages.

m_josenhans@ raises a good point: the dependencies brought with JAVA and WX may not be wanted. Debian resolved this by splitting Erlang in many packages.

I sometimes use the WX tools, never the JAVA option. I don't have any strong opinions on both options. Until we have subpackages, we could make slave ports for those two options, even if this means building Erlang 3 times for ports users.

The option that matters more to me is HIPE. What do you think about it?

The current package contains half of the hipe application, which is unusable and misleading (as said above, this is a known problem of the Erlang dsitribution, not FreeBSD). If the HIPE option is unselected, we should remove those files from the stagedir IMHO.

I have no experience with HiPE on non-amd64 archs, but I never had a single problem with it on amd64. I believe we could enable it on amd64.
